entry.title: one time, when it all goes wrong
entry.summary:

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 59,727Chapters: 11/?Kudos: 38

Summary: Beatrice is trying, all things considered. her family feels like it's breaking apart. Clark won't speak to her right now. and things keep happening around her.she needs to help everyone around her, the same way they need to help her.before they can't anymore. i don't own any of the characters and i don't give permission for AI to use my work

entry.title: Two Months In Kansas
entry.summary:
Author: Superbat_fan02

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,061Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 51

Summary: Alfred forces Bruce to spend two months on vacation in Kansas, a small, sparsely populated town, to give him a break from his grueling training and the mental exhaustion of spending almost every night at his computer reading thousands of Gotham police reports. On this short trip, Bruce meets Clark, a boy a year younger than him who is his complete opposite. Clark is cheerful, honest, empathetic, and responsible. The golden boy of Kansas, always with a smile and a pleasant tone of voice. But Bruce doesn’t believe that’s the whole story. Clark lives on and grew up on the farm where there are reports of a UFO crash, and, mysteriously, his “parents”—who weren’t parents at the time—are now his parents. Was it really a coincidence that Clark appeared after this supposed UFO news? Furthermore, not to mention that Bruce was already considered tall and well-built for his age, Clark was even taller and bigger than him. He always seemed to hear more than he intended to, or would carelessly lift objects that would be a challenge even for adults to lift. Bruce was sure of it—there was something about Clark, and he wanted to find out what it was.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Makeover
entry.summary:
Author: AwkwardFortuna

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,079Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 54

Summary: “...You’re not gonna stab me with that pencil, are you?” Clark’s voice cuts through the quiet, warm with something like amusement. He reaches out on instinct, fingers gently curling around Bruce’s pencil-wielding wrist, stilling him. Bruce doesn’t even flinch. Clark touches him with a kind of familiarity that he's never experienced before and it's... nice. Clark's touch doesn't jolt him like the others' do. Doesn't make his skin grow cold or his shoulders tense. And Bruce would never admit it out loud, but some days he only feels human when Clark is touching him. “It’s not a pencil,” Bruce says flatly. “It’s eyeliner.” A beat. “Besides,” Bruce adds, finally risking a glance at him. “I saw you swallow a bullet yesterday.”   Or, Bruce does Clark’s eyeliner.

entry.title: It’s brutal out here!
entry.summary:
Author: bigmouthstrikes_again

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,809Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 151

Summary: When Bruce’s silence gestured for him to go on, Clark chuckled slightly, fiddling with the sleeve of his shirt. “You know what I mean. Everyone had a grungy era of their childhood. Mine was just a little more . . . intense than others. I was more rebellious than troublesome, honestly.” Or Clark gets de-aged to seventeen a.k.a when he had his punk rock phase, and everyone is forced to bare witness

entry.title: Man of Steel, Heart of Glass
entry.summary:
Author: glitter_gutz

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,393Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 34

Summary: Bruce hates how Clark treats him like a petulant child, but he craves the adrenaline of being with the city's golden boy. Their intimacy is a battlefield. And it's marked by biting, scratching, and grip so tight it bruises. Proud marks that prove they were the ones to break the other’s composure. It’s a toxic, addictive cycle where the only time they feel truly close is when they are tearing each other apart and putting themselves back together.

entry.title: Second time is the charm
entry.summary:
Author: Emelian65

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 32,048Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 287

Summary: Clark wakes up in his bed, feeling strange. He had vivid dreams of a cataclysmic future. He sits up, tries to shake it off, but the memories are too detailed, overwhelming. He remembers the world ending, heroes falling, his own death? But then he's alive, young. He checks the date... It's now 2011, several years in the past.

entry.title: In The Wee Small Hours Of The Morning
entry.summary:
Author: fartsmellyman

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 32,066Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 100

Summary: He pressed his thumb against the card in his pocket. It moved over the embossed letters in an absent act of reverence. Bruce Wayne. He'd call about the interview, he decided. That was the right thing. The professional thing to do for both of them. He would call, and he would be a journalist, and he would not think about the bathroom or the heartbeat or the way Bruce had gone still and careful the moment Clark had pulled back, asking "Do you want to stop? " like what he wanted really mattered, even if Bruce looked like he wanted to take, like Clark's answer was the only important thing weighing on him.He would absolutely not think about that. Or: Superman and Batman meet properly for the first time; Clark does not handle it as gracefully as he should, the heartbeat memorization wasn't on purpose, and he would like everyone to stop looking at him like that.

entry.title: Simplest Words Make The Most Complicated Sentences
entry.summary:
Author: LuKuXing

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,449Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 90

Summary: In a world where your soulmate's first words were written on your body, Bruce couldn't think of anything other than how much he appreciated his soulmate, even if they weren't here. Soulmates were supposed to love you for who you were in your core. Would Bruce's soulmate love him as he is? Would they cherish him? Put up with him? ----- Clark never knew what it meant to have a soulmate. It was distant, something that always reminded him that he wasn't human. Being an outsider was all he was, as the people around him found their soulmates, talked about them like the best thing. Could he even ever feel that, If he doesn't have a soulmate?

entry.title: The Moment I Knew (I'd No Choice But To Love You)
entry.summary:
Author: erzisnottonfire

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,409Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 49

Summary: “What about you?” Clark asked, his fingers stopping momentarily in their idle tracing of Bruce’s mosaic of scars. Bruce could feel those impossibly blue eyes staring at him, and he lifted his head slightly to meet his gaze. Bruce blinked once. And then again, realising he had no idea what was being asked of him. He mumbled out a tired “…what about me?” when his brain has managed to connect to his mouth long enough to get the words out and tried not to shy away from the inexplicably adoring look sent his way in response. “When did you realise you liked me as more than a… coworker? a friend?” He supplied, his fingers resuming their previous task, the man clearly unable to stay still for longer than a few minutes at a time. Bruce would envy his seemingly endless energy if it wasn’t currently standing in the way of his date with at least six hours of consecutive sleep.   OR: Clark asks Bruce when he fell in love with him, Bruce reminisces on the memory while considering how far he had come since. Title (and most of the plot) from Abstract (Psychopomp) by Hozier

entry.title: Penitent
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 210,491Chapters: 21/21Kudos: 24

Summary: Bruce Wayne has been many things. Heir. Vigilante. Billionaire. The particular kind of broken that wears control like armor. When he contracts with professional Dom Clark Kent through The Agency, he's looking for structure. A container. Something to hold the edges of a life that has been running at catastrophic load for twenty years. What Clark is looking for is more complicated. What Clark finds is more complicated still. Penitent is the story of two men doing difficult work — some of it in session, most of it in the spaces between. It moves non-linearly through Bruce's eleven-month contract, Clark's formation as a Dom and survivor, and the slow, brutal, necessary business of one person learning to return another person's body and mind to themselves. This is not a love story disguised as kink. This is kink as the mechanism by which a love story becomes possible. Read Supplicant first. Not optional.

entry.title: Bring the Bird Home
entry.summary:
Author: dolcesher

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,288Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 77

Summary: There had been one universe they’d visited in which he and Clark were together. Married. But Bruce refused to think about it. Everything was easier that way.Except now stood a child that seemed to be from a universe much like that one, where he and Clark were not just together, but parents.An eleven year old Dick Grayson is mysteriously transported from a parallel universe where he was adopted by Bruce and Clark as a baby. While Bruce takes care of him, he examines his relationship with his sons, and a certain Kryptonian that won't leave him alone.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Oh sweet, sweet chestnut jelly
entry.summary:
Author: SolventBoy

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,984Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 72

Summary: He’ll be here, Clark thought, forcing his shoulders to relax. He wouldn’t have suggested this place if he didn't want to come. He looked around the room, eyes wide. Desperate to find a distraction—to do anything other than stare at the empty chair in front of him.
